articles.title: تامر حسني يفاجئ جمهوره ويطرح ألبومه كاملا

articles.title: Capital Pay to continue digital revenue collection under new cash payment directive
articles.summary: The national government has said Capital Pay will continue operating in the country’s digital revenue collection system as authorities introduce a cash-based payment arrangement aimed at addressing a shortage of physical currency. articles.context: The introduction of a cash payment directive comes amid ongoing economic challenges in South Sudan, exacerbated by hyperinflation and a shortage of physical currency that has persisted since the civil war began in 2013. The government’s reliance on digital revenue collection through Capital Pay reflects efforts to stabilize the economy and improve tax collection, particularly following the 2021 revitalized peace agreement aimed at fostering economic reforms and governance.

The handover, facilitated by local authorities, reflects efforts to restore peace and trust in the region, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at stabilizing the country.

articles.context: تأتي تصريحات فرح الديباني في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واجتماعية كبيرة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. يعكس عرض "داليدا.. الأغنية التي لا تنتهي" الحاجة إلى الفنون كوسيلة للتعبير عن المشاعر الإنسانية في ظل الأزمات، مما يساهم في تعزيز الروح الثقافية والتضامن بين السودانيين في هذه الأوقات الصعبة.

articles.title: WBGs police urge residents to collect 10,000 unclaimed nationality certificates
articles.summary: Police in Western Bahr el Ghazal State have urged residents to collect their processed Nationality Certificates, warning that thousands of documents remain unclaimed. articles.context: The issuance of Nationality Certificates in South Sudan has become increasingly significant following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to stabilize the country post-civil war. With ongoing challenges related to citizenship and identity, the unclaimed certificates highlight the difficulties residents face in accessing essential services and rights, exacerbated by the political instability and economic hardships that continue to affect the region.

articles.title: Sudan reports suspected cholera cases in 4 states
articles.summary: Health Ministry says cases detected in North Kordofan, West Kordofan, South Darfur, Khartoum as humanitarian crisis deepens amid ongoing war
articles.context: Sudan has been engulfed in conflict since April 2023, when fighting erupted between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread displacement and a humanitarian crisis. The ongoing violence has disrupted health services and sanitation, exacerbating the risk of cholera outbreaks. The recent reports of suspected cholera cases in North Kordofan, West Kordofan, South Darfur, and Khartoum highlight the dire public health challenges amid the deteriorating security situation.

articles.title: Besigye Treason Case Takes New Twist as Key Audio Evidence Reportedly Disappears

articles.context: The treason case against Dr. Kizza Besigye, a prominent Ugandan opposition leader and four-time presidential candidate, has intensified amid ongoing political tensions in Uganda, particularly following the controversial 2021 elections. articles.title: One Dead, Three Injured in Fatal Crash Along Mbale–Tirinyi Highway

The ongoing instability in Western Bahr el Ghazal, marked by frequent clashes and displacement, highlights the challenges of implementing peace agreements and addressing local grievances, particularly as the country approaches the 2024 elections.

articles.context: Albert Ojwang's arrival at the Central Police Station is significant amid heightened tensions following the August 2022 elections in Kenya, which were marred by allegations of electoral misconduct. articles.title: Why teachers rejected Sh8bn salary increment

articles.context: The rejection of the Sh8 billion salary increment by teachers in Kenya comes amid ongoing tensions between the Kenya National Union of Teachers (KNUT) and the government over the implementation of the 2021-2025 Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA). This disagreement is exacerbated by the recent economic challenges facing the country, including inflation and budget cuts in the education sector, which have heightened teachers' demands for better compensation and working conditions.

His U-turn on the funding model is significant as it addresses the concerns raised by student unions and civil society groups, especially after the controversial 2021 budget cuts that affected university financing. This shift could reshape the political landscape ahead of the 2027 elections.

Additionally, the recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021 has bolstered regional connectivity, enhancing Kenya's position as a key aviation hub in East Africa.

Recent events, such as the controversial eviction of residents in Nairobi's Mukuru slum in early 2023, highlight the urgency of this issue.

The upcoming boycott reflects ongoing frustrations with the judicial system, especially as the country approaches the 2022 general elections, where fair judicial processes are critical for upholding democracy.

Her efforts came in the wake of the 2001 WHO report highlighting Nigeria as a hub for fake medications, which significantly impacted the fight against diseases like malaria and HIV/AIDS across the region.

The Commonwealth Games provide an opportunity for redemption and to showcase Kenya's athletic prowess on an international stage.

articles.title: Why WhatsApp usernames could upset Somalia's anti-terror fight

articles.context: Somalia has faced ongoing conflict since the civil war erupted in 1991, leading to the rise of militant groups like Al-Shabaab, which has exploited communication channels for recruitment and coordination. The Somali government, supported by international partners, has been intensifying anti-terror operations, particularly since the 2020 agreement with the African Union to bolster security forces. The introduction of WhatsApp usernames could complicate these efforts by allowing militants to communicate anonymously, undermining counter-terrorism strategies.

articles.title: تطور جديد في المحكمة الرياضية حول قضية بطل أفريقيا بين المغرب والسنغال
articles.summary: كشفت تقارير عن تأجيل الحكم المنتظر في القضية الخاصة بالنزاع القائم حول تحديد بطل نسخة كأس أمم أفريقيا الماضية. ووفقًا لما أورده موقع”IUSPORT” الإسباني المتخصص في القانون الرياضي، فإن الحكم الذي كان من المنتظر صدوره خلال شهر يوليو الجاري، قد يتأجل إلى شهر أكتوبر المقبل على أقرب تقدير، ما يعني استمرار حالة الترقب بشأن القضية. …
articles.context: تعود جذور النزاع حول بطل كأس أمم أفريقيا إلى المباراة النهائية التي أقيمت في فبراير 2022، حيث واجهت المغرب السنغال في مباراة مثيرة. بعد ادعاءات تتعلق بتحكيم المباراة، تقدم الطرفان بشكاوى إلى المحكمة الرياضية، مما أدى إلى حالة من التوتر بين البلدين. تأجيل الحكم إلى أكتوبر 2023 يعكس استمرار الخلافات الرياضية والسياسية بين المغرب والسنغال، ويزيد من تعقيد العلاقات بينهما في سياق المنافسات الإقليمية.

articles.title: Reflection on UK-Kenya one year later: A solid partnership that gets stuff done
articles.summary: Kenya and the United Kingdom share a deep and vibrant partnership, anchored in shared values...
articles.context: The UK-Kenya partnership has evolved significantly since the signing of the UK-Kenya Strategic Partnership in January 2021, which aimed to enhance cooperation in trade, security, and climate change. Amidst ongoing challenges such as regional instability from conflicts in Ethiopia and Somalia, this partnership is crucial for Kenya's economic recovery post-COVID-19 and its role as a stabilizing force in East Africa. The recent UK-Kenya trade agreement further solidifies this collaboration.

articles.title: Build better systems for diaspora remittance
articles.summary: Economists call these flows remittances, and they are anything but small.
articles.context: Kenya has seen a significant increase in remittances, which reached a record $3.7 billion in 2021, largely driven by its diaspora in the U.S. and Europe. This surge is crucial as the country grapples with economic challenges exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising inflation. The Kenyan government, under President William Ruto, is now prioritizing the formalization of remittance channels to enhance financial inclusion and economic stability, making this discussion particularly timely.

articles.title: Right to dignity in childbirth
articles.summary: That obligation is grounded in medical ethics, public health and constitutional law.
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2010 Constitution enshrined the right to health and dignity, yet maternal mortality rates remain alarmingly high, with 342 deaths per 100,000 live births as of 2020. Recent reports have highlighted systemic failures in healthcare delivery, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing political tensions surrounding healthcare funding. The government's commitment to the Universal Health Coverage program, initiated in 2018, faces scrutiny as disparities in maternal care persist across urban and rural areas.

articles.title: Enable funding for all eligible varsity students
articles.summary: Genuine concerns about the funding model should be addressed
articles.context: In Kenya, the Higher Education Loans Board (HELB) has faced criticism for its funding model, which many argue excludes deserving students. The ongoing debate intensified following the 2022 general elections, where education funding became a key issue. Additionally, the 2021 report by the Commission for University Education highlighted disparities in access to funding, prompting calls for reforms to ensure equitable support for all eligible varsity students, particularly from marginalized communities.

articles.title: SHA transparency vital
articles.summary: There is a need for full disclosure in contracts entered by SHA and other public entities.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has faced increasing scrutiny over public procurement processes, particularly following the 2018 scandal involving the National Youth Service, which revealed widespread corruption. The ongoing push for transparency is underscored by the government's commitment to the 2020 Public Procurement and Asset Disposal Act, aimed at enhancing accountability. As the State House Advisory (SHA) engages in new contracts, the demand for full disclosure is critical to restore public trust and combat corruption.

articles.title: محمد حماقى ينعى هانى شنودة: «مصر خسرت قامة فنية كبيرة»
articles.summary: نعى الفنان محمد حماقى الموسيقار هانى شنودة، الذى رحل عن عالمنا صباح اليوم عن عمر ناهز 83 عامًا، معربًا عن حزنه لفقدان أحد أبرز رموز الموسيقى المصرية. وقال محمد حماقى فى منشور له عبر حسابه بموقع التواصل الاجتماعى «فيس بوك»: «خالص العزاء لأسرة الموسيقار الكبير هانى شنودة.. مصر خسرت النهارده قامة فنية كبيرة، بس أكيد …
articles.context: The passing of هانى شنودة, a prominent figure in Egyptian music, comes at a time when the cultural landscape of the Horn of Africa is facing challenges due to political instability and social unrest. His contributions to the music scene, particularly during the 1970s and 1980s, helped shape modern Egyptian identity amidst regional tensions. As Sudan navigates its ongoing political crisis following the 2021 coup, the loss of such cultural icons underscores the importance of artistic heritage in fostering unity and resilience.

articles.title: Ethiopian police arrest alleged leader of multi-million-dollar trafficking ring
articles.summary: Mohammed Tahir has yet to comment on allegations he led a multi-million-dollar ring using torture to get money.
articles.context: The arrest of Mohammed Tahir comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing struggle with human trafficking, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which has destabilized the region and increased vulnerability among displaced populations. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been under pressure to address human rights abuses, particularly following the 2022 joint report by the UN and Ethiopian Human Rights Commission highlighting widespread trafficking and exploitation.

articles.title: CES cabinet approves SSP 94 billion 2026-2027 budget
articles.summary: The Central Equatoria State (CES) Council of Ministers has approved spending ceilings exceeding SSP 94 billion for the 2026–2027 fiscal year. In a post on the Official Facebook Page of the Office of the state governor on Tuesday, the decision was reached during an extraordinary Cabinet meeting chaired by Governor Emmanuel Adil Anthony on Tuesday. […] The post CES cabinet approves SSP 94 billion 2026-2027 budget appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The approval of the SSP 94 billion budget comes amid ongoing economic challenges in South Sudan, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war and subsequent peace agreements, including the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS). Governor Emmanuel Adil Anthony's leadership is crucial as the state grapples with issues of governance and resource allocation, particularly in a region where local conflicts and economic instability persist.

articles.title: Ministerial committee directs cash tax payments to ease liquidity crisis
articles.summary: The Ministerial High-Level Committee on Economic Reform has directed all taxpayers to pay their taxes in cash into the bank accounts of the South Sudan Revenue Authority (SSRA) as part of measures aimed at addressing the country’s growing liquidity challenges. According to a statement shared on the Ministry of Finance’s Facebook page, the directive was […] The post Ministerial committee directs cash tax payments to ease liquidity crisis appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced severe economic challenges since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by ongoing conflicts and political instability, particularly the civil war from 2013 to 2018. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict aimed to establish peace but has struggled to stabilize the economy. The current liquidity crisis, worsened by global economic pressures and internal mismanagement, has prompted the Ministerial High-Level Committee to implement cash tax payments to bolster government revenue.

articles.title: Sh4.3bn Grace Ogot estate in EACC recovery suit
articles.summary: Commission seeking cancellation of all titles arising from the disputed transactions.
articles.context: The Grace Ogot estate, valued at Sh4.3 billion, has become embroiled in a legal battle as the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) seeks to annul titles linked to alleged fraudulent transactions. This case highlights ongoing issues of land corruption in Kenya, exacerbated by the 2010 Constitution's devolution efforts, which aimed to address historical injustices. The estate's significance is further underscored by its connection to the late author and politician Grace Ogot, a prominent figure in Kenyan history.

articles.title: Thugge seeks power over terrorism funding in microfinance Bill
articles.summary: CBK says the Microfinance Bill omits key anti-money laundering provisions.
articles.context: The Microfinance Bill comes amid heightened concerns over terrorism financing in Kenya, particularly following the 2019 attack on the DusitD2 hotel in Nairobi, which underscored vulnerabilities in financial oversight. The Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) has been under pressure to strengthen anti-money laundering measures, especially after the Financial Action Task Force's 2021 recommendations. This bill's shortcomings could hinder efforts to combat financial crimes linked to groups like Al-Shabaab, complicating Kenya's security landscape.

articles.title: بعد ساعات من خسارة كأس العالم.. حساب الكرة الذهبية يوجه رسالة مفاجئة إلى ميسي
articles.summary: قام الحساب الرسمي لجائزة الكرة الذهبية (Ballon d’Or) ) بتوجيه رسالة مؤثرة إلى النجم الأرجنتيني ليونيل ميسي، عقب خسارة منتخب بلاده أمام إسبانيا بهدف دون رد في نهائي كأس العالم 2026 أمس الأحد ، في رسالة اعتبرها كثيرون تكريمًا لمسيرة أحد أعظم لاعبي كرة القدم عبر التاريخ. ونشر الحساب الرسمي للجائزة عبر منصاته على مواقع …
articles.context: تأتي خسارة الأرجنتين في نهائي كأس العالم 2026 في وقت حساس، حيث يعاني العالم من توترات سياسية واقتصادية متزايدة، بما في ذلك الأزمات في منطقة القرن الأفريقي. كما أن ميسي، الذي يُعتبر رمزًا عالميًا، يمثل الأمل والتفاؤل في أوقات الشدائد، مما يجعل تكريمه من قبل حساب الكرة الذهبية يعكس أهمية الرياضة في توحيد الشعوب وتعزيز الروح الإيجابية في ظل الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: Ruto names Charles Owino government spokesperson in Executive shake-up
articles.summary: Isaac Mwaura moved to head diversity office.
articles.context: The appointment of Charles Owino as government spokesperson comes amid President William Ruto's efforts to consolidate power following his election in August 2022. This shake-up occurs against a backdrop of ongoing political tensions with the opposition, particularly Raila Odinga's Azimio coalition, which has been vocal in its criticism of Ruto's administration. Additionally, Isaac Mwaura's transition to head the diversity office reflects Ruto's commitment to inclusivity, a key promise during his campaign.

articles.title: Ruto: Lenient courts embolden goons
articles.summary: The president said courts are releasing suspects on low bond terms.
articles.context: President William Ruto's comments come amid rising concerns over crime rates in Kenya, particularly following the 2022 elections, which were marred by violence and political tension. His administration has faced criticism for its handling of security issues, and the judiciary's perceived leniency, highlighted by the 2021 High Court ruling on bail reforms, has sparked debates about the balance between justice and public safety. This situation is further complicated by ongoing political rivalries and calls for judicial reform.

articles.title: Turkish naval vessels escort drilling fleet off Somalia
articles.summary: TCG Goksu, TCG Gelibolu, TCG Bartin provide protection for Cagri Bey drilling ship, support vessels
articles.context: The presence of Turkish naval vessels escorting the Cagri Bey drilling ship off the coast of Somalia highlights Turkey's growing influence in the region, particularly following the 2020 maritime agreement between Turkey and the Somali government. This agreement aimed to enhance cooperation in resource exploration amid ongoing tensions with other nations, including Kenya, over maritime boundaries. The situation is further complicated by Somalia's internal instability and the resurgence of piracy, which threatens foreign investments.

articles.title: Japan heat wave claims seven lives, thousands hospitalized
articles.summary: A severe heat wave sweeping across Japan has left seven people dead and sent thousands more to hospitals, according to government data released this week. According Kyodo News, the Fire and Disaster Management Agency said 4,580 people were taken to hospitals by ambulance for heat-related illnesses between July 6 and July 12. The figure marks […] The post Japan heat wave claims seven lives, thousands hospitalized appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: While the article focuses on Japan, the significance of the heat wave resonates globally, particularly in regions like South Sudan, which has faced extreme weather patterns exacerbated by climate change. The ongoing conflict since the South Sudanese Civil War (2013-2018) has hindered the country's ability to respond to climate-related disasters, leaving communities vulnerable. As nations grapple with climate impacts, the need for international cooperation and support for affected regions becomes increasingly urgent.

articles.title: Volleyball: Kenya's finest players honoured
articles.summary: KPA, Pipeline stars sweep top volleyball league awards.
articles.context: Kenya's volleyball scene has gained prominence following the national team's strong performance in the African Volleyball Championships, where they secured a bronze medal in September 2023. This achievement has reignited interest in the sport, highlighting the contributions of clubs like Kenya Pipeline and Kenya Ports Authority (KPA) in nurturing talent. The recognition of top players comes at a time when the government is investing in sports development to foster national pride and unity.

articles.title: Wau police investigate teacher’s killing, second fatal shooting, and boy’s drowning
articles.summary: Police in Wau, Western Bahr el Ghazal State, are investigating three separate incidents that claimed the lives of two men, including a prison officer and teacher, as well as a 15-year-old boy on Monday. Speaking to Eye Radio on Tuesday, Wau Police Spokesperson Santino Udol said officers first responded to a reported shooting in the […] The post Wau police investigate teacher’s killing, second fatal shooting, and boy’s drowning appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The recent incidents in Wau occur against the backdrop of ongoing instability in South Sudan, where violence remains prevalent despite the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil war. The killing of a teacher and a prison officer highlights the challenges of law enforcement and governance in a region still grappling with the aftermath of conflict, as well as the urgent need for security reforms to protect civilians and public servants alike.

articles.title: Drug mule busted at Mombasa Airport after swallowing 44 cocaine pellets
articles.summary: How a 67-year-old allegedly tried to smuggle cocaine out of Kenya.
articles.context: Kenya has become a significant transit hub for drug trafficking due to its strategic location along major shipping routes. The rise in drug-related crimes, particularly involving international cartels, has prompted the Kenyan government to intensify its anti-narcotics efforts, especially following the 2021 Narcotics Control Act aimed at curbing drug abuse and trafficking. This incident underscores the ongoing challenges faced by authorities in combating drug smuggling, particularly as the region grapples with increasing drug-related violence and corruption.

articles.title: عمرو دياب يطرح ألبومه المنتظر حبيتك غدا عبر المنصات الموسيقية المختلفة
articles.summary: أعلن النجم عمرو دياب عن طرح ألبومه الجديد حبيتك غدا الأربعاء في تمام الساعة 5 مساء على كل المنصات الموسيقية المختلفة، وذلك من خلال حسابه عبر موقع التواصل الاجتماعي فيس بوك. ويتعاون عمرو دياب خلال الألبوم الجديد مع عدد كبير من الشعراء والملحنين، ويجمع الألبوم بين أكثر من نوع موسيقي مختلف. وكان عمرو دياب طرح …
articles.context: عمرو دياب، المعروف بلقب "الهضبة"، يعد أحد أبرز الفنانين في العالم العربي، وقد أثرى الساحة الموسيقية بأعماله منذ التسعينيات. يأتي إصدار ألبومه الجديد "حبيتك" في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات سياسية واقتصادية كبيرة، مما يجعل الموسيقى والفن وسيلة للتعبير عن الأمل والتغيير. في ظل الأزمات المستمرة، يمكن أن يسهم الألبوم في تعزيز الروابط الثقافية بين الفنانين والجمهور في المنطقة.

articles.title: وزير البنى التحتية بشمال كردفان يبحث مع وحدة السدود خطة تأهيل مصادر المياه بالأبيض
articles.summary: بحث وزير البنى التحتية والتنمية العمرانية المكلف بولاية شمال كردفان، المهندس معاوية آدم، اليوم، مع وفد وحدة تنفيذ السدود بوزارة الزراعة والري ، سبل دعم جهود الولاية لمعالجة قضايا المياه بمدينة الأبيض، وذلك بحضور مدير قطاع المياه بالولاية ومدير مياه الأبيض. وأوضح وفد وحدة السدود، برئاسة المهندس عبدالرحيم الأمين عبدالله، مدير إدارة الإشراف والمتابعة، أن …
articles.context: تواجه ولاية شمال كردفان، وخاصة مدينة الأبيض، تحديات كبيرة في إدارة موارد المياه بسبب التغيرات المناخية والصراعات المحلية. في عام 2021، تم توقيع اتفاقية سلام جوبا، والتي تهدف إلى معالجة قضايا التنمية والبنية التحتية في المناطق المتأثرة بالنزاع. تأتي هذه المناقشات في وقت حرج حيث تسعى الحكومة الانتقالية لتعزيز الاستقرار وتحسين الخدمات الأساسية في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية والسياسية المستمرة.

articles.title: بحث قضايا التنمية والخدمات بولاية نهر النيل
articles.summary: انعقد اليوم بمقر صندوق التنمية المحلية بولاية نهر النيل اجتماعاً مشتركاً برئاسة المدير التنفيذي لصندوق التنمية المحلية الدكتور عوض الكريم علي المبارك والمدير التنفيذي لمحلية الدامر والأستاذ عبد الباسط محمد الأمين بحضور نائب المدير التنفيذي للصندوق الأستاذ محمد أحمد حمدو ومدير الشؤون المالية والإدارية الأستاذ معاوية عباس ومدير التنمية بمحلية الدامر ومشرف المحلية بالصندوق وبحث …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الاجتماعات في سياق جهود الحكومة السودانية لتعزيز التنمية المحلية في ظل التحديات الاقتصادية والسياسية التي تواجه البلاد بعد الثورة في 2019. يعاني السودان من أزمات متعددة، بما في ذلك النزاعات المسلحة في مناطق مثل دارفور، مما يزيد من أهمية تحسين الخدمات الأساسية في الولايات مثل نهر النيل. كما أن وجود صندوق التنمية المحلية يعكس التزام الحكومة بتعزيز الاستقرار والتنمية في المناطق النائية.

articles.title: تنفيذي رشاد يخاطب ورشة تقنيات إنتاج الصمغ العربي الجاف والنقي
articles.summary: خاطب المدير التنفيذي لمحافظة رشاد ولاية جنوب كردفان جعفر القاسم الورشة التدريبية حول تقنيات إنتاج صمغ عربي جاف ونظيف والتي تنظمها منظمة الأغذية والزراعة العالمية (الفاو) التابعة للأمم المتحدة بالتعاون مع معهد بحوث الصمغ العربي ودراسات التصحر بجامعة كردفان ضمن مشروع الاصماغ من أجل التكيف والتغيير المناخي بالسودان. وشارك في الجلسة الافتتاحية السادة أعضاء اللجنة …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الورشة في وقت حرج لسودان، حيث يعاني من آثار النزاع المستمر منذ عام 2019، والذي أثر على الاقتصاد والزراعة. تسعى الحكومة الانتقالية، بدعم من منظمة الفاو، إلى تعزيز إنتاج الصمغ العربي كوسيلة لتحسين سبل العيش وتعزيز الأمن الغذائي. كما أن المشروع يهدف إلى مواجهة التغير المناخي، مما يجعله جزءاً من الجهود الأوسع لتحقيق الاستقرار في ولاية جنوب كردفان.

articles.summary: أكد الأستاذ الطاهر إبراهيم الخير والي ولاية الجزيرة لدى لقائه وفد منطقة ام القرى اليوم، أن مطالب اللجنة العليا لتنمية وإعمار محلية أم القرى الخدمية والتنموية من أولويات برامج حكومة الولاية وهي حق وليست منحة . معلناً دعمه وتبنيه لنفرة تأهيل الطريق الرابط أم القرى بالطريق القومي (مدني _ بورتسودان) . إستعرض الأستاذ محمد عثمان …
articles.context: The local government in Sudan has faced significant challenges in infrastructure and service delivery, particularly in rural areas like Um al-Qura. The region has been affected by ongoing economic difficulties and political instability since the ousting of former President Omar al-Bashir in 2019. The commitment by the governor, الطاهر إبراهيم الخير, to prioritize development in Um al-Qura reflects a broader effort to address local grievances and improve governance amid rising public demands for better services.

articles.title: Warrap State condemns killing of a teacher in Wau
articles.summary: The Government of Warrap State has strongly condemned the killing of Ustaz Michael Majok Madut The post Warrap State condemns killing of a teacher in Wau appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .

The government's condemnation reflects a broader concern over the safety of educators and the need for peace in a region still grappling with the aftermath of the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, aimed at ending years of conflict.

articles.title: Mbadi rules out enhanced borrowing despite reduced revenue projection
articles.summary: The CS also ruled out the possibility of introducing new tax measures.
articles.context: Kenya's economy has faced significant challenges due to the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing drought conditions, which have strained public finances. The government's decision to avoid enhanced borrowing comes amid a projected revenue shortfall for the 2023-2024 fiscal year, as outlined in the National Treasury's budget estimates released in April 2023. This stance is particularly significant as it reflects the government's commitment to fiscal discipline amidst rising public debt, currently exceeding 60% of GDP.

articles.title: Police urge Juba residents to report alleged motorcycle robberies
articles.summary: Police are urging residents of Juba to report criminal incidents after concerns emerged over armed individuals riding motorcycles who allegedly stop motorists and rob civilians in parts of the city. The concerns follow reports from several residents who said they were followed and confronted by armed men along Airport Road. One victim, identified as Yitjuok, […] The post Police urge Juba residents to report alleged motorcycle robberies appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The rise in motorcycle robberies in Juba comes amid ongoing instability in South Sudan, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war and the fragile peace agreement signed in 2018 between the government and opposition groups. The country continues to grapple with high crime rates and insecurity, particularly in urban areas, as the government struggles to maintain law and order amidst economic challenges and political tensions.

articles.title: Higher education minister asks private universities to review tax laws
articles.summary: The Minister of Higher Education has directed vice chancellors of private universities to review the 2024/2025 Appropriation Bill and submit recommendations aimed at addressing taxation concerns and improving government support for higher education institutions. In a posting on his Facebook page on Monday, Minister Changkuoth Bichiock Reth issued the directive during a meeting with leaders […] The post Higher education minister asks private universities to review tax laws appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan's higher education sector has faced significant challenges since the country's independence in 2011, including limited government funding and ongoing economic instability exacerbated by civil conflict. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to foster stability, yet issues like taxation for private universities remain contentious. Minister Changkuoth Bichiock Reth's call for a review of tax laws reflects the urgent need for reform to support educational institutions amid these ongoing challenges.

articles.title: مدير شرطة نهر النيل يعلن عن افتتاح وحدات لحماية الأسرة والطفل بكل المحليات
articles.summary: اكد اللواء شرطة حقوقي دكتور قرشي السر مدير عام شرطة ولاية نهر النيل أن التدريب يمثل حجر الزاوية للتطور والتعلم واكتساب الخبرات، مشدداً على أهمية تضافر الجهود لترسيخ مبدأ حماية الأسرة والطفل. جاء ذلك لدى مخاطبته اليوم بقاعة دار الشرطة بعطبرة فعاليات ختام الدورة التدريبية للعدالة الجنائية للأسرة والطفل، والتي نظمتها إدارة حماية الأسرة والطفل …
articles.context: The establishment of family and child protection units in the Nile River State comes amid ongoing efforts in Sudan to reform the police and judicial systems following the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. This initiative aligns with the transitional government's commitment to enhancing human rights and addressing gender-based violence, particularly in light of the increased vulnerabilities faced by women and children during the protracted political instability and conflict in the region.

articles.title: US renews Iran strikes; Trump cites soldier deaths
articles.summary: The US military says it launched another round of strikes against Iran to “further degrade” its capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The 10th consecutive night of strikes took place after US President Donald Trump vowed to punish Iran for the deaths of US soldiers. In response, Iran said it struck two oil […] The post US renews Iran strikes; Trump cites soldier deaths appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The recent escalation follows the 2020 assassination of Iranian General Qassem Soleimani by a US drone strike, which heightened tensions between the two nations. articles.context: The breach of Kenyan President William Ruto's official website comes amid heightened concerns over cybersecurity in the region, particularly following the 2022 general elections, which were marred by allegations of misinformation and digital manipulation. Kenya's increasing reliance on digital platforms for governance and public engagement has made it a target for cybercriminals, raising alarms about the integrity of state communications and the potential for political destabilization in a country that has historically faced electoral violence.

This transformation aims to streamline access to academic credentials for graduates, particularly those from conflict-affected regions, enhancing their employability and educational opportunities in a country striving for stability and development.

articles.title: Is regulation needed for electronic taxi-hailing companies in Ethiopia?
articles.summary: Over the past eight years, Ethiopia’s electronic taxi-hailing industry has grown remarkably. More than 50 companies entered the market, attracted by rising smartphone adoption, urbanization and changing consumer expectations. Yet today, fewer than 10 sizable operators remain, competing intensely for market leadership while most earlier entrants have disappeared. This raises an important policy question: Has […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's electronic taxi-hailing industry emerged amid rapid urbanization and the government's push for digital transformation, particularly following the 2018 reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. However, ongoing political tensions, including ethnic conflicts and the Tigray War, have destabilized the economy, leading to regulatory uncertainties. The recent 2021 Addis Ababa City Administration's transport policy, which aimed to formalize the sector, highlights the urgent need for regulation to ensure fair competition and consumer protection in this evolving market.

articles.title: From Kampala’s Streets to the World Cup Stage! Ghetto Kids Set to Shine With Shakira Before Billions

The group's rise from the streets of Kampala symbolizes resilience and hope for many Ugandans, especially youth facing limited opportunities amid ongoing social and political tensions in the region.

articles.title: Thousands Gather in Addis Ababa for Long-Awaited National Dialogue
articles.summary: Starting Wednesday, over 4,000 participants have gathered in Addis Ababa for the final conference of Ethiopia’s National Dialogue. The forum kicked off at the Addis International Convention Center and is scheduled to continue for the next several weeks. Its launch follows years of input collection by the National Dialogue Commission (NDC), which was established in […]
articles.context: The National Dialogue is a crucial step for Ethiopia following a devastating civil conflict that erupted in November 2020, primarily involving the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) and the federal government. The peace agreement signed in November 2022 aimed to end hostilities and foster reconciliation. This dialogue seeks to address underlying grievances and promote unity amid ongoing ethnic tensions and political fragmentation, making the current gathering in Addis Ababa particularly significant for the country's stability.

articles.title: Outstanding National Dialogue Agendas
articles.summary: Agenda Item 1: Nation-Building Issues of Identity and National Ownership/Belonging History and Identity Shared National Identity and Values (including value-related matters) Nation-Building and Diversity Management Language Matters Preservation of Culture, History, and Heritage, among other related issues Agenda Item 2: State Structure, Governance Form, Political System, Political Representation, and Electoral Systems State Structure […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and led to significant humanitarian crises and political fragmentation. The ongoing national dialogue, initiated in 2022, aims to address deep-seated issues of identity and governance, particularly in light of ethnic federalism's challenges and the need for inclusive representation. Key actors include the Ethiopian government, regional states, and various ethnic groups, all seeking a cohesive national identity amidst ongoing tensions.

articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges following the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, leading to disruptions in the oil supply chain and increased inflation. The government's decision to recapitalize the Ethiopian Petroleum Supply Enterprise comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize the economy and meet IMF conditions for financial support, particularly after the recent fifth review of the IMF program in October 2023.

articles.title: Who will win the final? Predictions, analysis, and match previews from MELBET experts

articles.context: As Ethiopia navigates a fragile peace following the Tigray conflict, the nation's passion for football serves as a unifying force amidst ongoing political tensions. The recent normalization of relations with Eritrea and the establishment of a new government structure have reignited hopes for national cohesion. This tournament, showcasing global football talent, provides a backdrop for Ethiopians to rally around their favorite teams, fostering a sense of community and national pride during a tumultuous period.

articles.title: TPLF Slams Planned Addis Protest, Accuses Organizers of Serving Federal Agenda
articles.summary: Council says rally seeks to halt renewed war, end alleged forced conscription, and enforce the Pretoria Agreement The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) has rejected a public rally planned in Addis Ababa by the Tigray Peace and Change Council, accusing its organizers of ‘serving the federal government agenda’. Meanwhile, the council—a coalition of Tigrayan opposition […]
articles.context: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the Ethiopian federal government since November 2020, leading to a humanitarian crisis in the Tigray region. The Pretoria Agreement, signed in November 2022, aimed to cease hostilities, but tensions remain high, particularly regarding issues like forced conscription. The Tigray Peace and Change Council's planned protest reflects ongoing divisions within Tigrayan politics and the struggle for representation amidst federal control.

articles.title: Ministry Orders Competitive National Search for University Presidents
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education has replaced the appointment system for leaders of Ethiopia’s public universities with a nationwide competitive recruitment process. The Ministry now requires all presidential and vice presidential vacancies to be publicly advertised, evaluated through standardized merit-based criteria, and filled through an open selection process involving university governing boards, independent committees and the […]
articles.context: The shift to a competitive recruitment process for university leaders comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing political reforms initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, aimed at decentralizing power and enhancing institutional integrity. This move follows years of ethnic tensions and protests over university governance, particularly after the 2020 Tigray conflict, which highlighted the need for more transparent and accountable leadership in educational institutions. The new policy seeks to stabilize and professionalize university administration across the country.

articles.title: Building Fiscal Strength Without Breaking Society
articles.summary: On July 1, 2026, the Executive Board of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) officially completed its fifth review of Ethiopia’s USD 3.4 billion Extended Credit Facility (ECF) arrangement, triggering an immediate disbursement of approximately USD 464 million. For macroeconomic planners in Addis Ababa, the announcement was a moment of administrative triumph, signaling continued compliance with […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant challenges since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which exacerbated economic instability and humanitarian crises. The government’s commitment to the IMF's Extended Credit Facility, initiated in December 2021, reflects its efforts to stabilize the economy while navigating complex political dynamics, including ongoing tensions with Tigray and other regions. The recent disbursement is crucial for funding recovery and rebuilding efforts amid these challenges.

articles.title: IMF Predicts Wait for Foreign Bank Entry, Forward FX Market, NBE Gold Exit
articles.summary: Chartered flights transport gold to Dubai Ethiopian authorities and the IMF underline the need to reinforce the National Bank of Ethiopia’s role in gold purchase and forex sales in light of slow progress in market maturity. The policy shift is highlighted in the fifth review of the IMF program disclosed this week, which contrasts with […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges following the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, leading to disruptions in trade and foreign investment. The National Bank of Ethiopia's (NBE) role in managing foreign exchange and gold reserves has become increasingly critical as the country seeks to stabilize its economy amid ongoing inflation and currency devaluation. The IMF's fifth review of its program, released in October 2023, emphasizes the urgent need for reforms in these areas.

articles.title: Issues Unresolved by National Dialogue will Head to Referendum: Commission
articles.summary: Issues that fail to find resolution through the national dialogue conference will head to referendum, according to the National Dialogue Commission. The Commission has introduced a document that details procedural issues on how decisions will be handled following the conclusions of the conference that kicked off this week at Addis International Convention Center. Over around […]
articles.context: The national dialogue conference in Ethiopia, initiated on October 2, 2023, aims to address ongoing ethnic tensions and political fragmentation exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The National Dialogue Commission, established in 2021, seeks to foster reconciliation among various political factions, including the Prosperity Party and opposition groups. The potential for unresolved issues to be decided by referendum highlights the urgency of finding consensus amid Ethiopia's complex political landscape.

articles.title: East African Community Postpones Currency Launch to 2031
articles.summary: The East African Community (EAC) has set 2031 as the new target for launching its long-planned single regional currency, after delays caused by the COVID-19 pandemic and wider economic challenges. EAC officials said preparations are continuing, with a phased rollout expected once member states, including Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania, meet the required economic convergence criteria. […]
articles.context: The EAC's plan for a single currency dates back to the 2013 EAC Monetary Union Protocol, aiming to enhance regional trade and economic stability. However, the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing economic challenges, including inflation and currency fluctuations, have hindered progress. The postponement to 2031 reflects the need for member states like Kenya and Uganda to achieve the required economic convergence criteria, particularly in light of recent tensions over trade imbalances and fiscal policies within the region.

articles.title: City Cracks Down on Property Fraud
articles.summary: The Addis Ababa City Administration has begun digitizing land certificates and adopted a new rule for automatically dismissing employees implicated in land fraud, officials told the City Council this week. “We have digitized all the titles created up to now, and we hold the information as scanned digital backups. Because of the system we have […]
articles.context: Addis Ababa has faced significant challenges with property fraud, exacerbated by rapid urbanization and a lack of transparent land management systems. The government's push for digitization follows a 2021 report by the Ethiopian Human Rights Commission highlighting widespread corruption in land allocation. This initiative is part of broader reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration aimed at restoring public trust and stabilizing the economy amidst ongoing political tensions and ethnic conflicts in the region.

articles.title: Germany Holds First Strategic Peace and Security Discussion with African Union
articles.summary: In a historic step toward deepening continental cooperation, the Federal Republic of Germany and the African Union (AU) have officially launched their first-ever strategic dialogue on foreign and security policy at the AU Headquarters in Addis Ababa. The milestone consultations, held during an official visit on July 13 and 14, 2026, were led by German […]
articles.context: The strategic dialogue comes amid increasing geopolitical tensions in the Horn of Africa, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict and ongoing instability in Somalia and Sudan. Germany's engagement with the African Union reflects a broader European interest in enhancing security partnerships in the region, especially following the AU's role in mediating conflicts and promoting peace initiatives, such as the African Union's Silencing the Guns initiative launched in 2013.

articles.title: Former Climate Summit President Says Ethiopia Must Deliver Results at COP32
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s hosting of the 32nd United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP32) should be judged by concrete action rather than new climate pledges, says Laurent Fabius, former French foreign minister who chaired the landmark 2015 Paris Climate Conference. Speaking in Addis Ababa during a visit to support Ethiopia’s preparations for the summit, Fabius said the international […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's hosting of COP32 comes amid ongoing tensions related to the Tigray conflict, which has drawn international attention and criticism regarding human rights and environmental impacts. The country is also grappling with the effects of climate change, including droughts and food insecurity, which have been exacerbated by the conflict. Fabius's emphasis on actionable outcomes reflects a growing urgency for Ethiopia to demonstrate its commitment to climate resilience and sustainable development in this challenging context.

articles.title: No Place to Feel Free: Young Tigrayans Caught Between Fear, Survival, and Hope
articles.summary: At just 19 years old, Kansu Ferede has already lost her childhood, had her education cut short, and suffered a painful separation from her family. Sitting in a quiet, anonymous corner of Addis Ababa—hundreds of kilometers away from her hometown of Humera—she speaks of the one thing she wants most, yet has rarely known: peace. […]
articles.context: The Tigray War, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian federal government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), has devastated the Tigray region, leading to widespread displacement and humanitarian crises. Despite a peace agreement signed in November 2022, many Tigrayans, like Kansu Ferede, continue to face fear and uncertainty as tensions persist and recovery remains slow, highlighting the ongoing struggles of youth in a region still grappling with the aftermath of conflict.

articles.title: “The World is Changing Rapidly”: Senior German Diplomat on Next Chapter of Cooperation with Ethiopia
articles.summary: Diplomatic ties between Ethiopia and Germany stretch back more than a century, with both countries opening embassies in the early years of the 20th century. Today, Germany is a key trade and development partner to Ethiopia, accounting for a large proportion of the country’s coffee exports and investing heavily in humanitarian and development initiatives through […]
articles.context: Ethiopia and Germany have maintained diplomatic relations since 1905, but recent years have been marked by Ethiopia's internal conflict, particularly the Tigray War that began in November 2020. Germany has been actively involved in humanitarian efforts, responding to the crisis and supporting peace initiatives, including the Pretoria Agreement signed in November 2022. This backdrop underscores the significance of Germany's role as a development partner amid Ethiopia's ongoing political and social challenges.

articles.title: From Water to Wellness: Bridging the Survival Gap in Rural Ethiopia
articles.summary: For Beshir Alnadir, a quiet resident of Bambasi Woreda in the western borderlands of Benishangul-Gumuz Regional State, watching his toddler daughter smile is nothing short of a miracle. Not long ago, her eyes were vacant, her fragile body was wasting away, and her life was hanging by a thread. Born healthy, her world fractured at […]
articles.context: Bambasi Woreda, located in the Benishangul-Gumuz region, has faced significant challenges due to ongoing ethnic tensions and conflict exacerbated by the 2020 Tigray War. The region has experienced disruptions in humanitarian aid and access to clean water, impacting child health and nutrition. Recent government initiatives, such as the 2022 National Nutrition Strategy, aim to address malnutrition, making stories like Beshir Alnadir's particularly poignant as they highlight the urgent need for effective interventions in rural areas.

articles.title: The Midnight Synthesizer: How Teddy Mak’s Curfew-Era Defiance Rewrote the Sound of Ethiopia
articles.summary: Some nights in 1980s Addis Ababa, the music had to wait for the city to go quiet. High up in the restaurant of the Wabi Shebelle Hotel, past the military curfew and empty streets patrolled by the ruthless Derg regime, a group of teenagers huddled around a synthesizer and a drum machine. They had to […]
articles.context: In the 1980s, Ethiopia was under the oppressive rule of the Derg regime, which enforced a brutal military curfew amid widespread civil unrest and famine. This period saw significant resistance from youth, particularly in urban centers like Addis Ababa, where cultural expressions became acts of defiance. The emergence of synthesizer music during this time not only challenged the regime's control but also laid the groundwork for Ethiopia's modern music scene, reflecting the resilience of its people.

articles.title: Inaugural Adama Bike Race revives Ethiopian cycling
articles.summary: The inaugural Adama Bike Race took place on July 11, 2026 in Adama, Ethiopia, aiming to revive the country’s cycling culture and position the city as a sport tourism destination. articles.context: Ethiopia has a rich cycling history, but the sport has waned due to ongoing political unrest and conflict, particularly since the Tigray War began in November 2020. The Adama Bike Race, held in a city known for its strategic location and cultural heritage, represents a concerted effort by the Adama City Administration and regional tourism bodies to revitalize local sports and promote peace through community engagement and tourism, amid a backdrop of recovery and reconciliation.

articles.summary: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.
articles.context: The resurgence of piracy off the coast of Yemen is linked to the ongoing instability in Somalia, where the militant group Al-Shabaab continues to exploit the power vacuum. The 2012 London Conference on Somalia aimed to address piracy through international cooperation, but recent events, including the hijacking of the tanker in October 2023, highlight the failure to secure maritime routes. This resurgence poses threats to regional trade and maritime security, drawing attention to the need for renewed international efforts.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s largest wind power project advances following $110 million AfDB funding approval

The financing package, sanctioned by the Bank’s Board of Directors, comprises $80 million from the African Development Bank, $20 million […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been striving to enhance its energy infrastructure amid ongoing regional tensions and internal conflicts, particularly following the Tigray War, which began in November 2020. The Aysha wind power project is part of Ethiopia's broader efforts to diversify its energy sources and reduce reliance on hydropower, especially as climate change impacts water availability. This financing from the AfDB, approved in October 2023, marks a critical step in achieving the country's ambitious renewable energy targets.

articles.title: NBE Account Number Issuance Service for New Importers, Exporters Goes Digital

Ambassador Massinga's focus on trade and healthcare reflects the U.S. commitment to stabilizing the region while addressing humanitarian needs exacerbated by the conflict.
